quotes.text: Churn is the silent killer. You can celebrate new customers all day, but if the bucket has holes, you're just wasting water.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Churn is the tax you pay for not making your product indispensable.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The SaaS customer lifecycle: sign up, use it twice, forget password, reset password, never log in again. Your job is to break this cycle.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: SaaS metrics that matter: retention, retention, retention. Everything else is a vanity metric you report to investors.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The 'cancel flow' is where SaaS companies reveal their true character. Make it easy and people will come back. Make it hard and they'll never come back.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The most important user is the one who's about to leave. Build for them first.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your first customer is worth more than your next thousand. Treat them that way.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: SaaS churn is like a leaky bucket. You can pour water in as fast as you want, but if the holes are big, you're wasting time.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your best customers aren't the ones who pay the most. They're the ones who stay the longest and tell others.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your SaaS should be so good that canceling feels like a loss, not a relief.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your most valuable product feedback comes from the users who almost left but stayed. Find out why they stayed.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to retain talent is to give them meaningful work, not just a good salary.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your growth rate matters less than your retention rate. A leaky bucket can't be filled.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your most valuable customers are the ones who stick around. Retention is the ultimate metric.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your customer's journey doesn't end at the sale. It starts there.
quotes.author: based
